name: netprobe
summary: Repository indexing entry for the ``netprobe`` project. Index entry in the KFIOSA catalog. Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner. Attribution and provenance only; audit code, releases, and licence before use in the lab or production environment.

usage_sections.content: sudo python3 main.py -t 192.168.1.0/24 -m scan  (interactive: sudo python3 main.py; dashboard: python3 main.py --dashboard)

examples.command: sudo python3 main.py -t 192.168.1.0/24 -m scan  (interactive: sudo python3 main.py; dashboard: python3 main.py --dashboard)

algorithms: ARP subnet sweep for live-host enumeration (scanner.py)
TCP port scanning with service identification (port_scanner.py)
MAC OUI vendor lookup + hostname reverse-DNS pattern matching for device-type fingerprinting (fingerprint.py)
ARP table inconsistency monitoring for spoof/MITM detection (arp_detector.py)
periodic ARP rescan 'monitor mode' to flag newly-joined hosts (alerts.py + whitelist.py)
network topology visualization + HTML/PDF/JSON report generation
produces: Flask web dashboard with network topology map
novelty: none for WiFi recon — this is a wired/LAN-style ARP host enumerator and port scanner, not an 802.11 frame tool. It does not use aircrack-ng, airodump-ng, wash, hcxtools, or scapy 802.11 monitor-mode capture; its "monitor mode" is just periodic ARP rescans, not radio monitor mode. No WPS, PMKID, handshake, EAPOL, deauth, hidden-SSID, or GPS capability. The integration of ARP sweep + port scan + OUI fingerprint + Flask dashboard + Telegram alerts is a generic home-network inventory tool; KFIOSA's existing scapy/airodump baseline already covers host enumeration more directly at the 802.11 layer.

trust.warning: External community repo. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use. Catalogued for the AI's awareness; novel algorithms are implemented in core/modules/catalog_recon.py, not shelled out to this repo's binaries.

use_cases: Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Indexing only — no automated invocation. The operator must manually inspect the repository before adding to a toolchain.
